title: Brockton man facing 14 charges after allegedly engaging in shootout with police
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jailson Andrade, 44, faces 14 charges after a shootout with Brockton police, including assault and firearm offenses.
Andrade allegedly engaged in a shootout after a high-speed chase, resulting in non-life threatening injuries to him and a bystander.
Police recovered a 9mm pistol with an obliterated serial number at the scene; Andrade held without bail pending a hearing.

title: 1 killed, 1 injured in Charlton crash, driver reportedly refused to stop for police
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A fatal crash on Route 20 in Charlton killed one driver and injured another on Tuesday morning.
The crash involved a speeding pick-up truck that reportedly refused to stop for Charlton police.
The highway was closed at the Charlton and Sturbridge town line; investigation is ongoing by state and local police.

title: Victim identified in fatal Roxbury hit-and-run
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Steven Stewart, 47, from Worcester, was identified as the victim in a fatal Roxbury hit-and-run on March 20.
The incident occurred near Southampton Street and Bradston Street; the driver fled the scene, according to authorities.
No arrests have been made; Boston police continue to investigate the Roxbury hit-and-run case.

title: Mike Vrabel reacts after TreVeyon Henderson lends support to Bulls guard cut after anti-LGBTQ+ statements
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Mike Vrabel addressed TreVeyon Henderson's social media posts supporting Jaden Ivey after anti-LGBTQ+ remarks led to Ivey's release from the Bulls.
Vrabel emphasized the importance of Patriots players expressing beliefs responsibly and representing the team and organization inclusively.
Jaden Ivey was released by the Bulls for conduct detrimental to the team after making anti-LGBTQ+ comments on Instagram.

title: This Concord mom is making her dream come true by running Boston
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Kelly Turner, 43, from Concord, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for the New England Patriots Foundation.
Turner's journey includes overcoming challenges as a single mom and battling Lyme disease before pursuing her marathon dream.
The New England Patriots Foundation supports underserved communities, aligning with Turner's personal and community-focused marathon goals.

title: She’s running Boston in memory of her best friend
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Brette Miller is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of her friend Angelle Lozano, who died from a brain aneurysm.
Miller, from Dallas, Texas, is participating to support The Brain Aneurysm Foundation's mission of improving early detection and research.
Approximately 30,000 people in the U.S. suffer a brain aneurysm rupture each year, with a high mortality rate.

title: Clover Food Lab puts itself on the market
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Clover Food Lab is seeking a buyer after filing a notice to lay off 182 workers, complying with state regulations.
Clover operates 11 restaurants, a meal box delivery, and catering business; it no longer has any food trucks.
Clover emerged from bankruptcy in 2024, with plans to add 50 new locations across New England in five years.

title: ‘BPS transportation is failing us’: School bus delays have Boston parents calling for change
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston Public Schools' bus system is facing delays, with many students arriving late due to inconsistent service.
The district's buses failed to meet the 95% on-time benchmark, averaging 88% in the afternoons in March.
BPS spends over $200 million annually on transportation, yet parents frequently coordinate alternative plans due to missed trips.

title: How did the ‘Seger bar’ guy get Gov. Healey for his latest video?
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey appeared in Brian Daly's viral video series about bars playing Bob Seger music.
Healey joined Daly at 21st Amendment in Beacon Hill, where they ordered a Guinness and listened to Seger.
Their meeting was coincidental, as Daly encountered Healey while filming, leading to her spontaneous guest appearance.

title: Michelin-recommended dumpling chain Nan Xiang to open in Framingham
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Nan Xiang Soup Dumplings plans to open its first Massachusetts location in Framingham later this year.
The restaurant will be located at 84 Worcester Rd., taking over the former Fresh Market space.
Nan Xiang is part of a chain known for handmade dumplings and has been Michelin-recommended multiple times.
